$schema_note: GOLDEN FIXTURE 3 of 4 (W1.23). Exercises the exceptions-queue ledger mechanics (SPEC 7.3 EXCEPTIONS; W1.19): capture with the raw payload preserved across ALL FIVE typed reasons (unroutable_missing_ids, unknown_anthology, stage_mismatch, tenant_mismatch, legacy_reconciliation), the participant-tied capture that stamps stage_cursor='exception', resolve, idempotent resolve replay (no-op), and the guard refusals (unknown reason -> exit 5, non-JSON raw payload -> exit 5, unknown exception id -> exit 3). fixtures/golden/replay_golden.py replays this; the W5.5 force-failure drills exercise the same capture/reconcile shape end to end. All data is synthetic: zero PII, zero secrets, zero client identifiers, zero Anthropic model ids.
title: Exceptions-queue capture and resolve across every typed reason
narrative: Five malformed or misrouted synthetic submissions land in the exceptions queue, one per typed reason, each preserving its raw payload. One tenant-mismatch capture is tied to a known participant, stamping that participant's cursor to 'exception'. The operator resolves the unroutable capture (and a replayed resolve is a no-op) and resolves the legacy_reconciliation capture to a real participant key. Guard refusals prove the queue never accepts an unknown reason, a non-JSON payload, or an unknown exception id.

steps.desc: create the participant used by the participant-tied capture
capture: a submission with no hidden ids (unroutable_missing_ids), raw payload preserved

body.note: form arrived with no contact_id / anthology_id / stage

steps.desc: capture: hidden ids present but the anthology is unregistered (unknown_anthology)
capture: a stage form arrives for a participant whose cursor does not match (stage_mismatch)

--raw-submission.note: s5 gate form arrived while cursor was s0_intake

steps.desc: capture: the payload location does not match the anthology registry binding (tenant_mismatch), tied to a known participant -> stamps stage_cursor='exception'
capture: a legacy_reconciliation entry (W1.19 reconciliation replay type)

--raw-submission.note: participant existed in the legacy flow; reconcile into the engine ledger

steps.desc: GUARD: an unknown reason is refused (not in the SPEC enum) -> validation error, nothing captured
GUARD: a non-JSON raw payload is refused (the payload must be preserved as valid JSON)
resolve replay is an acknowledged no-op (idempotent)
resolve the legacy_reconciliation capture to a real participant key (reconciliation replay outcome)
GUARD: resolving an unknown exception id is an unknown-key error
